exemplar.title: Investigating Pursuit Curves in Mathematical Models

detailedExemplarData.title: Investigating Pursuit Curves in Mathematical Models

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear aim and logical progression of derivations
Recognisable introduction, body and conclusion with helpful headings
Relevant figures and tables supporting the narrative
weaknesses: Occasional abrupt algebraic jumps and minor slips interrupt flow
Repeated background explanations lead to verbosity
Inconsistent formatting of captions and section titles
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent with clear logical development and good overall organization. However, unnecessary repetition in algebraic explanations and formatting inconsistencies prevent it from reaching the top band for conciseness and presentation polish. A band-3 award accurately reflects its strengths in structure and clarity while acknowledging areas for refinement.

strengths: Consistent and accurate use of vector notation, differentials and parameters
Effective deployment of multiple representations (diagrams, graphs, tables)
Clear exposition in most sections, with only occasional inconsistencies
weaknesses: Subscript styles (e.g. S_p vs. S_D) occasionally switch
Some axes in graphs are unlabelled, reducing interpretability
A few LaTeX tags and symbol definitions are left undefined
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are used correctly throughout the exploration, and the student effectively integrates diagrams, tables and graphs to support the analytical narrative. While minor inconsistencies in notation and labelling exist, the communication remains clear and mostly consistent, justifying a band-4 award.

strengths: Clear personal anecdote linking the studentâs experience to the problem context
Use of software tools (Maple, MATLAB, Desmos) demonstrates initiative
Parameter choices show a personal approach to model design
weaknesses: Engagement does not consistently drive the mathematical development
Independent thinking remains within a standard chase-curve framework
Creativity, though evident, is not sustained at an outstanding level
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: There is significant evidence of independent thought and a clear personal approach, particularly in the choice of context and tools. However, the exploration does not reach the depth of originality or creativity required for the top band, placing it firmly in band 2 for personal engagement.

strengths: Meaningful discussion of model limitations and suggestions for extensions
Some critical analysis of results through interpretation of parameter ranges
Clear connection between the mathematics and enhanced understanding in the conclusion
weaknesses: Reflection is confined primarily to the final section rather than woven throughout
Depth of critical analysis is limited in earlier sections
Connections to understanding could be more thoroughly integrated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student offers meaningful reflection on limitations, future enhancements and the mathematical insights gained. This reflection, while valuable, lacks the sustained critical depth and pervasiveness throughout the work needed for band 3, making band 2 the appropriate level.

strengths: Sophisticated formulation and solution of differential equations at HL level
Closed-form integration and derivation of the critical threshold k > 1.618 demonstrate rigorous mathematical thinking
Clear geometric interpretation alongside algebraic development
weaknesses: Minor typographical and derivative errors (e.g., du substitution and missing dx)
Small inaccuracies in numerical approximations (1.618 vs 1.62)
Occasional missing differentials in integral expressions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration uses highly sophisticated and rigorous course-level mathematics, integrating differential equations, nontrivial substitutions and logarithmic integration to derive a closed-form condition. Despite minor typographical slips and arithmetic imprecision, the depth and sophistication of the mathematical content justify the maximum band-6 award.

strandSelections.reasoning: By adapting the classic dog-and-rabbit chase to a bounded square and testing threshold values such as k â 1.618, the student shows significantâbut not outstandingâindependent thought beyond textbook replication.

The student demonstrates good conceptual understanding of pursuit geometry, differential-equation formation and parameter interpretation across the exploration, though the reasoning does not reach the level of rigorous proof or sophisticated generalisation required for the top band.

Most algebraic steps are valid and final conditions (k > 1.618) agree with the derived formula.  Minor typographical mistakes and a wrong substitution of S_R/S_Râ1/k show that accuracy is âmostly correctâ rather than flawless.

Most material is relevant to modelling a pursuit curve, but several paragraphs repeat background points or quote large passages of routine algebra.  The write-up therefore drifts into verbosity and includes some unnecessary explanations of basic steps, so it is mostly rather than fully concise.

Vector notation, differentials (dy/dx) and parameter symbols (k, A, S_R) are used correctly most of the time.  Subscripts occasionally switch style (S_p vs S_D) and LaTeX tags are sometimes left open, but overall the mathematical language is appropriate for HL level.

Explanations are usually understandable, but occasional symbol inconsistencies (S_n appears without definition) and typographical slips reduce overall clarity.  Therefore communication is mostly clear with some inconsistencies rather than fully consistent.

Differential-equation modelling, arc-length substitution and logarithmic integration are all at HL level and are applied in a purposeful way to answer the research question; the mathematics is relevant and generally effective, though not exceptionally sophisticated.

The student supports the analysis with diagrams of the square park, slope triangles, Desmos graphs for three parameter sets and a tabulated summary.  These multiple representations help the reader grasp the modelâs behaviour, even if the graphics are basic and axes unlabelled.

The conclusion comments on limitations (constant speed, instant reaction, flat terrain) and suggests extensions, showing meaningful reflection; however, the reflection is brief and not deeply threaded through the work.

Using software (Maple, MATLAB, Desmos) and deriving an analytical interception condition exhibits creativity, yet the modelling remains within a standard framework; initiative is significant but not outstanding.

The investigation is generally easy to follow: the student states a clear aim, works through a chain of derivations and finishes with tables, graphs and a conclusion.  Although some algebraic steps jump abruptly or contain minor slips, the argument never collapses and the reader can see how each section builds on the previous one.  Hence coherence and logical development are clear but not exemplary.

The student analyses when the dog succeeds by forming and solving an inequality and interpreting parameter ranges, which demonstrates some critique of the results, but the analysis is not fully deep or wide-ranging.

Reflection clearly links back to understanding of pursuit phenomena and acknowledges how the maths informed this understanding; nevertheless, the connection is explained only in final paragraphs rather than throughout.

There is a recognisable introduction, body and conclusion; headings such as Introduction, Methodology and Conclusion guide the reader, and figures are inserted in roughly the correct places.  However, long formula blocks break the flow, and pagination / image captions are occasionally disordered, so the organization is good rather than outstanding.

Personal interest is evident in the opening anecdote and in the choice of parameters linked to a âparkâ.  This personal approach drives the exploration, though it is not deeply woven into every stage.

annotations.comment: The title is concise and clearly communicates the focus on pursuit curves, engaging interest effectively.
The introductionâs personal anecdote vividly demonstrates genuine engagement and links personal experience to the mathematical exploration.
A sentence fragment ('By applying mathematical modelling. I aim toâ¦') interrupts coherence; consider rephrasing into a complete sentence for clarity.
The clear and concise Aim section effectively frames the investigationâs objectives, guiding the reader through specific research questions.
The definition k = velocity of rabbit/velocity of dog is clear, but later notation switches; maintain consistent subscripts (e.g., S_R and S_D) throughout.
Deriving the direction slope using relative positions provides a clear geometric interpretation, strengthening the mathematical development.
The tangent substitution is appropriate, but include a brief justification of the chosen substitution to strengthen the mathematical exposition.
The differential du = -1/4 dx is incorrect for u = 1 â x/A; the correct derivative is du = -1/A dx. Please rectify to ensure algebraic accuracy.
The methodology mentions secondary research and software tools broadly; specify which sources and precise simulation steps to strengthen reproducibility.
Which differential equations will be solved analytically versus numerically? Clarifying this distinction will improve the methodological rigour.
The diagram effectively illustrates initial positions but lacks axis labels and a scale; adding these will enhance interpretability.
Reflection on model limitations appears only briefly here; integrating critical analysis of the square boundary throughout would deepen reflection.
The heading '2.Instantaneous Decision-Making' is missing a space after the dash; consistent formatting of section titles will improve overall presentation.
The caption 'Graph1:' merges words without spacing and uses inconsistent capitalization; revise to 'Graph 1: Showing the pursuit curveâ¦' for clarity.
Equation (2) is correct, but reference to Wolfram and Lloydâs paper lacks full citation; provide authors and publication year in-text for credibility.
Figure 3 effectively visualizes the pursuit curve but could be improved by distinguishing trajectory lines and adding a legend for different parameter sets.
The phrase 'he suggest' is ambiguousâreplace pronoun with the authorâs name and correct verb tense to maintain academic tone.
The substitution T = 1/S_D â«ââ¦ lacks differential dx in the first integral sign; ensure all integral expressions include the differential for mathematical correctness.
The term â1+(F(x)) is missing the exponent 2 inside the square root; this typographical error affects the integrity of the differential equation.
The step isolating (AâX) dF/dx = (S_R/S_D)ââ¦ should explicitly show substitution of k = S_R/S_D; please detail this step to avoid confusion.
The pair of equations resetting the expression to zero and one is unclear; consider streamlining by stating the nontrivial branch directly for clarity.
Equation (6) neatly captures the pursuit curve solution in closed form, demonstrating a solid integration of differential equation methods.
The text references 'A!' after k and initial distance; verify whether '!' denotes factorial or a typographical error to avoid misinterpretation.
The inequality analysis to determine the critical threshold k > 1.618 is clearly presented and connects well to the physical interception condition.
When factoring k^2âkâ1 the root is approximately 1.618, not 1.62; using precise values or symbolic expressions would strengthen the mathematical accuracy.
Table 1 effectively summarizes intercept points but would benefit from consistent significant figures and clearly labelled units for readability.
Graph 2 shows the critical chase but omits axis ticks or labels, making quantitative interpretation difficult; include axes details next time.
In Graph 3, mark the interception point explicitly and add a legend to differentiate the dogâs and rabbitâs trajectories for clearer visual analysis.
The concluding sentence is cut off ('This visualisation revealsâ¦'); ensure all sentences are complete and check for truncation before final submission.
The conclusionâs discussion of future enhancements (3D motion, environmental factors) demonstrates substantial critical reflection and forward thinking.
References lack a consistent citation style and retrieval dates are missing for some sources; apply a uniform referencing format (e.g., APA) throughout.
